title: reseda luteola subsp. luteola
intro: Reseda luteola subsp. luteola, also known as dyer's rocket or weld, is a plant species in the borage family that has been used for centuries due to its medicinal and dye-producing properties. The plant contains a pigment called alkannin, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Reseda luteola subsp. luteola has been used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and salves, to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations. In addition to its medicinal uses, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to create a natural dye for fabrics, making it a versatile and valuable plant species.
